summary: Advanced Fourside stage decompilation by adding Fourside stage data/callback tables, replacing several placeholder functions with typed C implementations, and extending Ground per-stage union types for crane/UFO/helicopter behavior. The decomp.dev report recorded one new full match, grFourside_801F2DD0 (+236 bytes), and eight additional grfourside improvements, with grFourside_801F3274 reaching 89.15% and multiple setup/update helpers reaching about 99% matched. The rest of the PR was mostly support cleanup: exporting grAnime_801C7B24, correcting grFourside prototypes, adding callback/struct types, and applying formatting/include-order changes in item, menu, camera, and library files.
what_changed: Added Fourside static stage data in grfourside.c: grFs_803E3CE8, grFs_803E3CFC StageCallbacks, and grFs_803E3D94 StageData for "/GrFs.dat".

Expanded the Fourside parameter struct from two anonymous ints to named fields such as heli_wait, heli_wait_add, heli_stay_time, crane_wait, crane_iron_up/down min/max, crane_iron_spd, crane_iron_stop_acl, ufo_wait, ufo_cs_offs, ufo_stay_time, and ufo_challenge.

Implemented grFourside_801F2DD0 as a real Ground object factory/setup function returning HSD_GObj*, using Ground_801C14D0, GET_GROUND, GObj_SetupGXLink, callback dispatch, HSD_GObjProc_8038FD54, and an OSReport failure path.

Implemented several previously stubbed or partially undecompiled Fourside behavior functions including grFourside_801F2F34, grFourside_801F3154, grFourside_801F3274, grFourside_801F37FC, and grFourside_801F3B70.

Typed grFourside_801F30A0 as an mpLib collision callback taking Ground*, s32, CollData*, s32, mpLib_GroundEnum, and float instead of void* placeholders, while still using M2C_FIELD on CollData offset 0x34.
Added Fourside-specific Ground union structs in gr/types.h: grFourside_CraneVars, grFourside_UfoVars, and grFourside_GroundVars2, and kept grFourside_GroundVars for JObj pointers.
Changed Fourside code to distinguish gp->gv.fourside, gp->gv.foursideCrane, gp->gv.foursideUfo, and gp->gv.fourside2 instead of overloading a single generic fourside struct.
Exported grAnime_801C7B24 in gr/granime.h and removed/commented its static forward declaration in gr/granime.c so Fourside crane animation code could call it.
Updated grfourside.h prototypes from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS or void* signatures to typed signatures for grFourside_801F2DD0, grFourside_801F30A0, grFourside_801F3B70, and fn_801F3F74.
Performed broad formatting/include cleanups in item files, mnvibration.c, lbaudio_ax.c, camera.c, mncount.h, and related files, including pointer-cast spacing and ItemStateTable formatting.
smart_moves: The PR did not try to force all Fourside functions to full matches at once; it landed one complete match and several high-confidence near-matches, preserving placeholder markers where work remained.
Reconstructing the Fourside stage callback table and StageData upfront gave the decompiler enough real data context for both .data progress and behavior functions.
Splitting Ground union state into crane, UFO, helicopter-style, and JObj-pointer structs reduced misleading field reuse and made later Fourside functions easier to type.

The grFourside_801F2DD0 implementation mirrors the common stage object setup pattern: create ground gobj by id, install GX link, copy callbacks from StageCallbacks, register process callback, and report failure with file/line/id.
Named stage tuning fields from the external parameter block made logic like randomized crane waits and helicopter waits readable without having to fully name every per-Ground state field.
The mpLib callback signature for grFourside_801F30A0 is a useful step away from void* placeholders even though one CollData access still required M2C_FIELD.

Using existing stage/ground helper APIs such as Ground_801C2ED0, Ground_801C3FA4, Ground_801C4E70, mpJointSetCb1, mpLib_80057BC0, and Stage_GetCamBoundsTopOffset kept the implementation aligned with established engine patterns.
decomp_lessons: For stage files, matching often depends on reconstructing both code and data: StageCallbacks, StageData, and S16Vec3 arrays can unlock .data improvements and clarify which object id owns each callback.
When a stage has several independent map objects, separate Ground union structs are preferable to reusing one generic per-stage struct with contradictory field meanings.

Parameter/archive structs should be named around observed gameplay semantics when evidence is strong, such as crane_wait or ufo_cs_offs, but per-instance state can remain offset-like until behavior is fully understood.
Callback signatures matter for matching and readability. Replacing void* callback prototypes with Ground_GObj*, HSD_GObj*, CollData*, and mpLib_GroundEnum exposes the real caller contract.

Near-matching functions can still be valuable if they establish the state machine shape; grFourside_801F3274 documented the crane state transitions, animation calls, random waits, target Y calculation, and deceleration/bounce logic despite not being a full match.
Exporting an animation helper from grAnime should be done deliberately: grAnime_801C7B24 moved from a static-only declaration to a header declaration because stage code needed it.

Do not erase evidence of unresolved matching issues: the diff keeps comments and artifacts such as M2C_FIELD and a no-op expression, indicating parts of the translation were still matching-oriented rather than polished source.
naming_conventions: Fourside globals use the grFs_ prefix, e.g. grFs_804D69D8, grFs_803E3CE8, grFs_803E3CFC, and grFs_803E3D94.
Stage functions retain address-based names in the grFourside_801Fxxxx form until semantic names are known.
Ground union variants were named by subsystem or role: fourside, foursideCrane, foursideUfo, and fourside2.
External stage parameter fields were given gameplay-oriented snake_case names such as heli_wait, crane_iron_spd, crane_iron_stop_acl, ufo_wait, and ufo_cs_offs.
Unknown per-instance fields stayed offset-like, e.g. x0, x1.b0, x4, x8, xC, x10, which avoids overclaiming semantics.
Item state table globals retained address-based names such as it_803F79C0, it_803F7BC0, and it_803F7C00.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK(3 * 4) in grFourside_801F30A0, suggesting stack-shape preservation was needed for matching.
Used M2C_FIELD(r5, u8*, 0x34) to read a CollData field whose type/struct layout was not yet represented cleanly.
Used explicit function-pointer casts in camera.c, mnvibration.c, and gobj proc setup to satisfy current header/type mismatches while preserving call generation.
Matched grFourside_801F2DD0 fully by following canonical Ground object setup ordering: allocate/get object, zero callbacks, setup GX link, assign callback3, run callback0, then schedule callback2.

Kept exact constants in stage behavior code, such as callback link priority values, animation frames 0.0f/400.0f, crane timer 300, helicopter threshold 700, camera offsets -50/50/15/-15, and flag constants like 0xc0000000.
Modeled random wait/range calculations with HSD_Randi guarded by zero checks, matching common Melee patterns for optional random-add fields.
Implemented the crane as an explicit switch state machine over gp->gv.foursideCrane.x0, which is a reusable tactic for translating stage hazards with multiple timed phases.
Formatted function pointer declarations as int(*arr_4e4)[2] and casts as (s32(*)(Vec3*)) / (void (*)(int)) in places where style or formatter output affected diffs.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The decomp.dev bot reported matched code improvement to 50.34% (+0.01%, +236 bytes).
The bot reported one new full match: main/melee/gr/grfourside grFourside_801F2DD0, 236 bytes, 0.00% to 100.00%.

The bot reported eight improvements in unmatched grFourside functions/data, including grFourside_801F3274 to 89.15%, grFourside_801F2F34 to 99.87%, grFourside_801F3154 to 99.84%, grFourside_801F3B70 to 99.33%, and grFourside_801F37FC to 99.81%.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching grFourside_801F3274 to see how the crane state machine reached a full match.
Search for follow-up changes to grFourside_CraneVars and grFourside_UfoVars field names as semantics became clearer.
Check whether the grFourside_801F30A0 header address comment typo or signature was later corrected.
Look for later PRs replacing M2C_FIELD on CollData offset 0x34 with a real struct field.
Compare Fourside StageCallbacks and StageData setup against other stage files for reusable object factory patterns.
Search for later work on grAnime_801C7B24 after it was exported from grAnime.h.
